Operation 'approveConnectionFingerprint' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks details about what input is expected (fingerprint details in request body), what is returned (fingerprint object), and the side effect (enables fingerprint for authentication). Added these details for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Approves an SSH fingerprint for the specified connection.
data.description_suggestion: Approves an SSH fingerprint for the specified connection, enabling secure SSH connections. Requires the connection ID and fingerprint details in the request body. Returns the approved fingerprint object with status confirmation. This action enables the fingerprint for authentication on subsequent connection attempts.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about what input is expected (fingerprint details in request body), what is returned (fingerprint object), and the side effect (enables fingerprint for authentication). Added these details for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'revokeConnectionFingerprint' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is missing details about what is returned and the side effect (disables fingerprint for authentication). Added clarity about the response and the operational consequence of revocation for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Revokes an SSH fingerprint for the connection.
data.description_suggestion: Revokes an SSH fingerprint for the specified connection, disabling it for future authentication attempts. Requires the connection ID and fingerprint hash. Returns confirmation of the revocation. This action immediately prevents the fingerprint from being used for SSH authentication.
data.reasoning: Description is missing details about what is returned and the side effect (disables fingerprint for authentication). Added clarity about the response and the operational consequence of revocation for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Approves an SSL certificate for the specified connection.
data.description_suggestion: Approves an SSL certificate for the specified connection, enabling secure SSL/TLS connections. Requires the connection ID and certificate details in the request body. Returns the approved certificate object with status confirmation. This action enables the certificate for use on subsequent connection attempts.
data.reasoning: Description lacks details about what input is expected (certificate details in request body), what is returned (certificate object), and the side effect (enables certificate for SSL/TLS connections). Added these details for better AI agent understanding of operation usage and effects.

data.current_description: Revokes an SSL certificate for the connection.
data.description_suggestion: Revokes an SSL certificate for the specified connection, disabling it for future SSL/TLS connections. Requires the connection ID and certificate hash. Returns confirmation of the revocation. This action immediately prevents the certificate from being used for SSL/TLS authentication.
data.reasoning: Description is missing details about what is returned and the side effect (disables certificate for SSL/TLS connections). Added clarity about the response and the operational consequence of revocation for better AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all private links configured in your Fivetran account. Returns private link identifiers, names, regions, and configuration status. Use this operation to discover available private links for your connectors and destinations.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description is completely empty, which severely impacts AI agent interpretability. Added explanation of what the operation returns, pagination context, and use case guidance to help agents understand when to call this endpoint.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new private link configuration for secure connectivity between Fivetran and your data infrastructure. Requires specifying the region and display name. Returns the created private link object with a unique identifier and initial status. The private link becomes available for use with connectors and destinations after creation.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear. Description is empty, making it impossible for AI agents to understand required inputs or expected outcomes. Added details about required parameters (region, name), return value structure, and the side effect of the private link becoming available for use.
